title: Information in Cognizable Cases (FIR)
description: Every information relating to the commission of a cognizable offence, if given orally to an officer in charge of a police station, shall be reduced to writing. Section 173 BNSS governs the registration of a First Information Report (FIR). It permits filing an electronic FIR (e-FIR) and Zero FIR irrespective of territorial jurisdiction.
punishment: Procedural section mandating police duty to record complaints for cognizable offences.

title: Arrest by Police Officer and Notice of Appearance
description: Outlines circumstances under which police officers may arrest without warrant for cognizable offences punishable with 7 years or less. Mandates issuing a Notice of Appearance (Section 35(3) BNSS) before arrest unless arrest is necessary to prevent further offence or tampering of evidence.

title: Bail Provisions for Bailable and Non-Bailable Offences
description: Governs release of accused persons on bail. For bailable offences, bail is a matter of right. For non-bailable offences, bail is subject to judicial discretion based on nature of crime, severity of punishment, and likelihood of absconding.
punishment: Procedural provision for release on bond.

title: Forensic Investigation Requirement for Offences Punishable with 7+ Years
description: Mandates that for offences punishable with imprisonment for seven years or more, the officer in charge shall cause a forensic expert to visit the crime scene to collect evidence and videograph the process.
punishment: Mandatory forensic collection for serious offences.
